{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks which muscles pull the mandible back following a bilateral mandibular fracture in the canine region. From the retrieved documents, particularly Document [28], it is stated that bilateral mandibular fractures can result in retraction of the anterior fracture segment due to the action of the mylohyoid muscle, leading to airway compromise. This indicates that the mylohyoid muscle contributes to pulling the mandible back. Document [16] and [22] confirm that the mylohyoid muscle functions to depress the mandible, which is equivalent to pulling it backward. Additionally, Document [2] and [16] state that the genioglossus muscle is involved in the depression of the body of the tongue and possibly retrusion of the tip of the tongue, but not directly in pulling the mandible back. The masseter and temporalis muscles, as noted in Document [14], pull the posterior mandible upward, not backward. The thyrohyoid muscle (also known as stylohyoid) elevates and retracts the hyoid bone posteriorly, not the mandible. Therefore, the muscles that pull the mandible back are the genioglossus and mylohyoid. Option B matches this finding.", "answer_choice": "B"}
